name: Aarya Patel
title: CricBrain
reason: Rolled-back HEAD already contains the full fullstack build: /client (React+Vite, ~7 components incl. AISummary, AIInsightModal, TermExplainer, PlayerCard) and /server (Express + @google/generative-ai ^0.24.1). server/controllers/aiController.js has three real Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite endpoints (commentary with Analyst/Fanatic personas, insights in JSON mode, ELI5 explanation) with graceful mock fallbacks when genAI is null. README is detailed with architecture, routes, and quickstart. No live demo URL (form had none). Rolled-back state matches the submission's claims; not materially weaker than the post-event version.
ai_evidence: @google/generative-ai in server/package.json; gemini-3.1-flash-lite via getGenerativeModel in server/controllers/aiController.js (generateCommentary, generateInsights JSON-mode, generateExplanation); config in server/config/gemini.js

name: Deep Vadher
title: LifeQuest AI
reason: Rolled-back HEAD (`Final Demo Version with Responsiveness`) already contains all routes: quests, world, boss, coach, skilltree, futureself, domain, dashboard, stats, profile, plus cinematic components (AInarrator, CinematicIntro, ShadowMode, LevelUpCinematic). package.json has no AI SDK (no openai/gemini/anthropic). app/coach/page.js uses a hardcoded `aiResponses` object keyed by intent — the AI is canned strings, no fetch/axios calls. Live demo at lifequestai.netlify.app/login returns 200. Heavy framer-motion polish and RPG framing are real, but the agentic claim is essentially fake. Rolled-back state is consistent with the submission; not materially weaker.
ai_evidence: no AI code — hardcoded aiResponses literal in app/coach/page.js; no openai/gemini/anthropic SDK in package.json

name: Dwij Joshi
title: Nova Habit Tracker
reason: Rolled-back repo contains ONLY a default create-next-app README.md and nothing else — no package.json, no app/, no src/, no Firebase config, no AI code. The entire Next.js + TypeScript + Firebase + Gemini integration described in the submission was added in post-event commits. Live Vercel demo still returns 200 because Vercel is serving the post-deadline build, but the repo at 23:35 IST is effectively empty scaffolding. Rolled-back state is dramatically weaker than the submission described; substantive features and the Gemini chat endpoint (src/app/api/chat/route.ts) were added after the deadline.
ai_evidence: rolled-back repo has only README.md; no code, no SDK, no AI integration present at deadline

name: Sarvesh verma
title: Gamified habits
reason: Rolled-back HEAD (`last fixes`) has src/components/{Arena,Habits,Navigation,Dashboard}.jsx plus src/utils/gameLogic.jsx — small but functional vanilla React + Tailwind habit tracker. Dependencies are just react + canvas-confetti, zero AI SDK. README is still the default Vite template (the polished 'Protocol' README was added post-event). No live demo URL submitted on the form. Habit/streak/leaderboard mechanics fit the brief decently, but agentic theme is absent. Rolled-back state is materially weaker: README is default scaffolding instead of the custom one the post-event repo had, hurting quality.
ai_evidence: no AI code; deps are react + canvas-confetti only

name: Shlok Tilokani
title: IPL Dashboard
reason: Rolled-back HEAD already has the three-tier structure: data_generator/generator.py (103 LOC ball-by-ball sim), middle_layer/app.py (224 LOC Flask with pace/momentum/phase logic), frontend (HTML/CSS/JS, ~160 LOC). The .ai/ folder (memory.md, skills.md, implementationplan.json, handoff.md) is for the coding agent that built the app, not runtime AI. No AI imports anywhere. README is just `# GDG-APL-16052026` — one line; no run instructions present at deadline (the detailed README was added post-event). No live demo. Misses the agentic/AI theme almost entirely; rolled-back state is slightly weaker than the original pass on quality due to the minimal README.
ai_evidence: no AI code found; .ai/ folder is coding-agent context, not runtime AI

title: HabitForge

name: Zishan Vhora
title: Lockin
reason: Rolled-back HEAD (`vercel`) already contains the full product: 74 src files (React 19 + TanStack Router/Start, Radix UI, Supabase), three SQL migrations under supabase/migrations including 20260516151500_advanced_features.sql, polished doodle-aesthetic README with setup steps, Counter/Timer habits, Shield system, Battleground duels. Tech stack matches the form exactly. No AI SDK or LLM code anywhere — the 'agentic' claim refers to using AI tools (Antigravity/Claude/Stitch/Lovable) to write the app, which the rubric does not credit as runtime AI. No live demo URL submitted. Rolled-back state matches the submission; not materially weaker than what was originally reviewed.
ai_evidence: no AI code found; @supabase/supabase-js is the only external service; AI assistants used only as build-time vibe-coding partners

name: Bhavya Shah
title: NexusHabit
reason: Rolled-back repo at the 23:49 deadline contains literally one file: a `README.md` consisting of a single line `# Habit-tracker`. No HTML/CSS/JS, no Supabase config, no Gemini integration, no chatbot, no habit/streak/XP code — nothing the submission describes exists at the deadline. The single rolled-back commit (`Initial commit`) is just the GitHub-generated repo scaffold. Submission was made at 23:57 (after the 23:49 cutoff), and only one post-deadline commit was dropped during rollback, meaning the contestant pushed effectively no code before the cutoff. Rolled-back state is dramatically weaker than the submission described — there is no project here to evaluate.
ai_evidence: no AI code; repo contains only a one-line README at deadline

name: Aadi Shah
title: Beyond the Score
reason: Rolled-back HEAD (`Remove key`) is a flat dump of 16 JSX files (App.jsx, MatchScorecard, MatchAnalytics, MatchNarration, HeadToHead, VenueStats, etc.) in the repo root with no folder structure, no package.json, no vite/index.html, no public/, and crucially no `data/` directory — yet App.jsx imports from `./data/loadMatches`, `./data/loadPlayers`, `./data/glossary` and `./components/*`. The project is not runnable as-is at the deadline; 15 post-event commits were dropped, which presumably restructured it. MatchNarration.jsx contains a real Gemini 1.5 Flash fetch call but the API key constant is empty string and the file is the only AI integration. No live demo URL. Rolled-back state is materially weaker than the submission described.
ai_evidence: Gemini 1.5 Flash REST call in MatchNarration.jsx (key constant empty); no package.json or SDK declared

name: Pratham Raval
title: CRIQ
reason: Rolled-back HEAD (`Remove sensitive API key values from README`) already has the full stack present at the 23:49 deadline: backend/ FastAPI (main.py 177 LOC with 10+ routes — matches, summary, pressure, analysis, player stats, wagon wheel, matchup) plus backend/data.py (1041 LOC pandas analytics over IPL matches.csv/deliveries.csv); frontend/ React 18 + Vite + TS + Tailwind + d3 + recharts + zustand with pages (Home, MatchSummary, MatchAnalysis, Player, Team, FanTools) and 13 components (FanIQQuiz, WagonWheel, MomentumChart, PressureBar, WinProbChart, StatExplainer). frontend/src/utils/ai.ts wraps Gemini 2.0 Flash with caching and intelligent mock fallbacks, called from 5 surfaces (Home, MatchSummary, FanIQQuiz, MomentumChart, StatExplainer). Detailed 10KB README with quickstart. No live demo URL. Even after dropping 8 post-event commits, the in-window state is a polished, theme-fitting submission — not materially weaker than originally described.
ai_evidence: Gemini 2.0 Flash REST integration in frontend/src/utils/ai.ts with cache + mock fallback; consumed by FanIQQuiz.tsx, MomentumChart.tsx, StatExplainer.tsx, Home.tsx, MatchSummary.tsx; backend .env reads GEMINI_API_KEY (python-dotenv)
